message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "enchantment 5 sword (newcomer) → 145 G (100 + 30 ench + 10 first ins + 5 fee)"
Passing tests so far: 11

Add high-enchantment surcharge: items with enchantment ≥ 5 add 30% of the item's base premium. Like cursed, the first-insurance surcharge stays on the original base.

Expected: 100 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 145.

Must not break the earlier "enchantment 0 sword" (still 115) or "enchantment 3 cursed sword" (still 165).

Implement minimal. Run `pnpm test`. All 12 tests must pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and add the minimal code to pass the new test.
Now I'll add the high-enchantment surcharge. Minimal addition: for each item with `enchantment >= 5`, add 30% of base premium.

content.text:
All 12 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a check in the item-modifier loop in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts` — when `item.enchantment >= 5`, add 30% of the item's `BASE_PREMIUM` (mirrors the cursed-surcharge pattern).
- Result: All 12 tests pass (33 todo remain).
- Approach: Minimal — reused the existing per-item modifier loop and base-premium lookup. The surcharge is computed off the base premium (not the first-insurance-inflated total), so the earlier sword tests (enchantment 0 → 115; cursed enchantment 3 → 165) are unaffected. No refactoring performed.

Relevant files:
